Title: The miR164e–NAC32 module orchestrates maize plant height via post-translational regulation of DELLA protein stability

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Differentially expressed during internode elongation; reported as high in stems, roots, and grains for miR164 in maize background literature
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Manipulated in transgenic maize; functional effects assessed in above-ground internodes, especially the eighth internode and elongating internodes
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases plant height, ear height, internode length, and internode cell length; loss-of-function causes dwarfism and shorter internodes
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression and loss-of-function transgenics; RT-qPCR; scanning electron microscopy; dual-luciferase assay for target repression; immunoblotting of downstream ZmD8 abundance
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter for pre-miR164e in dual-luciferase assay; promoter for stable overexpression in maize not clearly stated
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: sRNA-seq identified miR164 family as internode elongation-responsive; no accession provided
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Natural field conditions in Beijing, China
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts upstream of ZmNAC32 and indirectly affects GA signaling without altering endogenous hormone contents; classified here as Common Gene because it is a miRNA rather than protein-coding pathway enzyme or TF
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for precision breeding of plant architecture and lodging-related internode traits via modulation of miRNA abundance
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmmiR164e positively regulates maize plant height by promoting internode and cell elongation. 2. It directly represses ZmNAC32 through sequence-specific cleavage/repression confirmed by dual-luciferase assay. 3. Through suppressing ZmNAC32, it reduces ZmD8 stability and enhances GA-responsive elongation without changing endogenous hormone levels.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcript abundance unchanged in ZmNAC32 transgenic materials; protein abundance altered in elongating internodes
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Protein detected in elongating internodes; involved in GA signaling during internode elongation
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: DELLA protein; GRAS family transcriptional regulator with DELLA domain and conserved C-terminal GRAS domain
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher ZmD8 stability is associated with reduced GA-responsive internode elongation and shorter plants
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Immunoblotting; Y2H; BiFC; LCI; pull-down assay; cell-free degradation assay; protoplast co-expression; site-directed mutagenesis of K399/K463
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq showed unchanged transcript abundance of ZmD8; no accession provided
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: GA signaling context; MG132 used in cell-free degradation control
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core mechanistic target of the module; K399 residue in the GRAS domain is required for interaction with ZmNAC32 and is implicated as a ubiquitination-associated site controlling stability; classified as Common Gene because it is a signaling repressor rather than a pathway enzyme or TF primary regulator in this study
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Protein stability interface, especially K399-dependent regulation, may be exploited to fine-tune plant height in breeding
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmD8 is a DELLA growth repressor whose protein stability is enhanced by ZmNAC32. 2. ZmNAC32 binds the GRAS domain of ZmD8 and requires residue K399 for interaction. 3. Stabilized ZmD8 suppresses GA-responsive cell wall gene expression and internode elongation.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in maize internodes; transcript decreases in miR164e overexpression lines and increases in miR164e loss-of-function lines
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Analyzed in elongating and basal eighth internodes; overexpression lines show strong functional effects on internode elongation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: GA-responsive cell wall remodeling and cell elongation via post-translational modulation of DELLA stability
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major negative regulator of internode elongation acting as the central switch between miR164e and DELLA stability
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Post-translational stabilization of DELLA protein ZmD8 through direct protein binding; indirect suppression of downstream GA-responsive cell wall genes
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: ZmD8 protein; downstream GA-responsive cell wall genes including XTH1; XTH5; XTH13; LAC11; LAC9; Bk2; PAL3/5; GUX1; GINT1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Does not appreciably alter GA biosynthesis/core signaling transcripts; associated with repression of downstream GA-responsive cell wall remodeling genes in overexpression lines
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms a protein complex with ZmD8
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression reduces plant height, ear height, internode length, and cell length; knockout modestly increases plant height through enhanced internode elongation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression lines; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout mutants; RT-qPCR; paraffin section cell measurements; RNA-seq; subcellular localization; Y2H; BiFC; LCI; Y1H; dual-luciferase promoter assay; immunoblotting; cell-free degradation assay; pull-down assay; protoplast co-expression; site-directed mutagenesis
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Stable maize overexpression promoter not specified; CaMV 35S used in subcellular localization and transient assays
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq transcriptome profiling of elongation zones of eighth internode on Illumina HiSeq 2500; no accession provided
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth at 28/22°C day/night with 12 h light/12 h dark for ZmNAC32 transgenics; GA signaling context
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Transcriptomic evidence indicates ZmNAC32 functions mainly through post-translational stabilization of ZmD8 rather than direct transcriptional activation of ZmD8; Y1H and LUC assays showed no direct binding/activation of ZmD8 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for precision breeding to fine-tune plant architecture and lodging resistance by modulating internode elongation; caution that overexpression causes dwarfing
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmNAC32 is the direct target of ZmmiR164e and acts as a negative regulator of maize internode elongation. 2. It physically interacts with the DELLA protein ZmD8 and stabilizes it post-translationally rather than by activating ZmD8 transcription. 3. Through ZmD8 stabilization, ZmNAC32 suppresses GA-responsive cell wall remodeling genes and reduces plant height.
